title: Dynamic Rendering Decision and Isolated Browser-Service Boundary · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs dynamic rendering decision and isolated browser-service boundary from revision, authorization, seed, URL, robots decision, request, response bytes, tree identity, record provenance, frontier transition, goroutine owner, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering.
Define typed request, origin policy, viewport, actions, readiness, budgets, response, errors, trace, and cancellation for a separate controlled service.
Apply authorization, SSRF, resource type, download, WebSocket, worker, and byte rules to every browser-initiated request.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Render Necessity · changed case · Review Render Necessity
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 4041 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove render necessity, typed browser port, subresource policy, untrusted results, and lifecycle evidence; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

options.text: The Browser Lifecycle Proof record says least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Browser Lifecycle Proof.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Browser Lifecycle Proof for least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract.
The record names the assumption, applies Browser Lifecycle Proof, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
